LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WOODRUFF-SAWYER & CO.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (FAC) IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP SEC 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE." COUNSEL'S DECLARATION SHOWS THAT THIS DID NOT OCCUR AS TO THE FAC. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO IMMEDIATELY MEET AND CONFER. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE FAC IS DUE BY AUGUST 24, 2020. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Dec-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Wesley Lowe, Esq., Mannion, Lowe & Oksenendler, wes@mlolawyers.com, 415-733-1050 for Plaintiff Richard Ghilotti, et al
Leighton Koberlein, Esq., Hansen, Kohls, Sommer & Jacob, LP., lkoberlein@hansenkohls.com, 916-781-2550, for Defendant Woodruff-Sawyer & Co.

Court provides further clarification of the Court's tentative ruling to counsel.

ARGUED. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WOODRUFF-SAWYER & CO.'s MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS:

Defendant Woodruff-Sawyer & Co.'s motion for stay is granted, effective March 19, 2021, under the court's inherent authority. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.) Defendant is an insurance broker. Plaintiff sued defendant 14 months ago in this court for negligently procuring automobile coverage. Related insurance-coverage litigation has been pending in Florida since 2014. Rather than move promptly for a stay here, defendant consumed this court's time and resources with two demurrers. Plaintiff's counsel insists he needs discovery right away, because memories are fading after seven years. But the Florida case has moved glacially due largely to plaintiff's unsuccessful appeals. Both parties are ordered to cooperate to complete all pending discovery by March 19, 2021; no further discovery may be propounded before the stay.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WOODRUFF-SAWYER & CO.'s DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT (FAC) is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 430.41, which requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone." Counsel's declaration shows that this did not occur as to the FAC. The parties are ordered to immediately meet and confer. Defendant's response to the FAC is due by August 24, 2020.
